sloka_910.meaning: by, under the guidance of a bona fide spiritual master, practice (of meditation on the Supreme Lord)

sloka_918.meaning: for spiritual success to attain the darśan or transcendental vision of the Supersoul

sloka_915.meaning: loss for him, he is not denied the chance of the fortune to see the Supersoul
loss for him, he is not denied the happiness of the heavenly plane

sloka_921.meaning: to the jñānī, the person of knowledge
to the karmī, person of action
to the performer of severe austerities (such as Chāndrāyaṇ)

sloka_883.meaning: For that person accomplished in yoga, that is, when he has achieved meditation

sloka_885.meaning: by the mind (enchanted by sense objects)
must not be made to fall down into the bondage of the material world
must be delivered from the bondage of the material world

sloka_892.meaning: at the top, between the eyes (centering the concentration)

sloka_902.meaning: free from the defects of attachment, fear, and anger
and situated in the spiritual nature
who is free from the action of the mode of passion

sloka_920.meaning: gains the perfection of many lifetimes’ endeavours
liberation in the form of both self-realisation and Supersoul-realisation
his consciousness cleansed of all worldly attachment

sloka_1336.meaning: and a heart that is never discouraged
state within which all association with unhappiness is severed

sloka_922.meaning: renders service to Me by engaging in the devotional practices based on hearing and chanting My glories
Of all types of yogīs on the paths of karma, jñān, tapasyā, aṣṭāṅga-yoga, bhakti, etc
with firm faith in the holy scriptures which corroborate the superexcellence of devotion for Me

sloka_886.meaning: for a person of uncontrolled mind

sloka_882.meaning: without renunciation of fruitive attachment (the basis of niṣkām karma-yoga)

sloka_911.meaning: by a person of uncontrolled mind
by the appropriate method of arduous practice (sādhana)

sloka_912.meaning: and whose mind deviates from yoga (due to a lack of practice and abnegation)
a person who engages in yoga practice due to his faith in yoga scriptures

sloka_909.meaning: powerful uncontrollable by even the intelligence
turbulent disturbing the intelligence, body, and senses

sloka_906.meaning: worships Me with devotion (based on hearing, chanting, and remembering)
situated in the heart of every being (as the Supersoul, My four-armed form measuring one prādeś, the distance between the thumb and forefinger)
in all circumstances (whether engaged in the execution of scripturally prescribed duties or not)

sloka_913.meaning: on the path of attaining the Absolute
and deviated from the paths of both karma and yoga

sloka_900.meaning: controlled by the yogic practice of dhāraṇā
completely steadying the mind within the self

sloka_891.meaning: of Kuśa grass, deerskin, and cloth
subjugating all mental and sensual activity
for self-purification, to attain spiritual realisation

sloka_895.meaning: whose effort in works is regulated
and whose sleeping and waking hours are in proper measure
For the person whose eating and recreation are regulated

sloka_916.meaning: given to pure and honest practices

sloka_884.meaning: to the objects of the senses – sound, touch, form, taste, and smell
and does not become attached to action in pursuit of their enjoyment
and renounces all thoughts of enjoyment
he is said to have attained to yoga

sloka_899.meaning: and by the mind (with the vision of the futility of mundane enjoyment)

sloka_887.meaning: yogī who has conquered the mind
of the peaceful (without attraction and aversion)
In cold and heat, happiness and unhappiness

sloka_881.meaning: nor is a yogī one who merely renounces physical activities
A sannyāsī is not one who merely renounces the fire sacrifice (agnihotra yajña) and other prescribed duties
a sannyāsī, one who has given up all for the Lord
and he is a yogī, one in union with the Lord

sloka_888.meaning: situated in the one unchanging spiritual nature
and of equal vision for earth, stone, and gold

sloka_905.meaning: to Me – he never fails to be conscious of Me

sloka_893.meaning: of emancipation by the destruction of material existence
of mind withdrawn from hankering for material enjoyment
engaging the mind in yogic meditation

sloka_904.meaning: and is a seer of the same spirituality everywhere
The yogī who is united with Brahma, the Absolute, who realises himself as qualitatively identical with Brahma

sloka_919.meaning: by his practice of the previous life

sloka_898.meaning: by the pure self of the same nature as the Supersoul
